                       Joseph M. Finerty, Editor
                       John E. Bregger, Associate Editor


                                    Editors' Note

                                       Beginning with the current issue, publication of data on employee compensation is
                                    being expanded. A new table (C-11) containing historical data for indexes of average
                                    hourly earnings excluding interindustry shifts and overtime has been added. In addition,
                                    tables C-12 through C-16 (formerly C-11 through C-15) and Chart 14 have been revised
                                    to include data for adjusted hourly earnings and hourly compensation in 1967 dollars.
                                    Historical data for average hourly earnings in manufacturing, excluding overtime, have
                                    been added to table C-1.

                                                               Establishment Data

                 COLLECTION                                                                  All national, State, and area employment, hours, earnings,
                                                                                         job vacancy, and labor turnover series are classified in accord-
                     Payroll reports provide current information on wage and             ance with the Standard Industrial Classification Manual, Bureau
                 salary employment, hours, earnings, job vacancies, and labor            of the Budget, 1967.
                 turnover in nonagricultural establishments, by industry and
                 geographic location.
                                                                                         Industry employment

                 Federal-State cooperation                                                   Employment data, except those for the Federal Government,
                                                                                         refer to persons oh establishment payrolls who received pay for
                     Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the             any part of the pay period which includes the 12th of the
                 respondent fills out a single employment or job vacancy-labor           month. For Federal Government establishments, employment
                 turnover reporting form, which is then used for national, State,        figures represent the number of persons who occupied positions
                 and area estimates. This eliminates duplicate reporting on the          on the last day of the calendar month. Intermittent workers are
                 part of respondents and, together with the use of identical             counted if they performed any service during the month.
                 techniques at the national and State levels, insures maximum
                                                                                             The data exclude proprietors, the self-employed, unpaid
                 comparability of estimates.
                                                                                         volunteer or family workers, farm workers, and domestic
                     State agencies mail the forms to the establishments and             workers in households. Salaried officers of corporations are
                 examine the returns for consistency, accuracy, and com-                 included. Government employment covers only civilian em-
                 pleteness. The States use the information to prepare State              ployees; military personnel are excluded.
                 and area series and then send the establishment data to the                 Persons on establishment payrolls who are on paid sick
                 BLS for use in preparing the national series.                           leave (when pay is received directly from the firm), on paid
                                                                                         holiday or paid vacation, or who work during a part of the
                                                                                         pay period and are unemployed or on strike during the rest
                 Shuttle schedules                                                       of the period, are counted as employed. Not counted as
                     Two types of data collection schedules are used: Form BLS           employed are persons who are laid off, on leave without
                 790—Monthly Report on Employment, Payroll, and Hours;                   pay, or on strike for the entire period or who are hired but
                 and Form DL 1219—Monthly Report on Job Openings and                     have not reported to work during the period.
                 Labor Turnover. These schedules are of the "shuttle" type,

                      Job vacancies are the stock of unfilled job openings as of the

                  for all kinds of positions, classifications and employment, full           Size and regional stratification
